title: Iran Begins Large Funeral Ceremonies for Late Supreme Leader
subtitle: Massive crowds gather in Tehran as the country honors Ayatollah Ali Khamenei four months after his death.

summary.en: Iran has started a multi-day funeral process for the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. The leader was killed in February during airstrikes by the United States and Israel. Huge crowds gathered in Tehran to mourn and show support for the government. Foreign leaders from several nations attended the early ceremonies to pay their respects. The event is being used by the Iranian government to demonstrate national unity and strength.
summary.zh: 伊朗已開始為已故最高領袖哈米尼舉行多日葬禮。該領袖於二月遭美國與以色列空襲身亡。德黑蘭聚集大批人潮哀悼並展現對政府的支持。多國外國領袖出席了早期的儀式以表達敬意。伊朗政府正利用此活動來展現國家團結與實力。

paragraphs.en: Massive crowds gathered in Tehran this week as Iran began a dayslong funeral for its late Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. The ceremonies, which are expected to last for several days, mark a significant moment for the country four months after Khamenei was killed in airstrikes launched by the United States and Israel. The event is designed to show that the Iranian government remains stable and popular despite the ongoing war.
paragraphs.zh: 本週德黑蘭湧現大批人潮，伊朗開始為已故最高領袖哈米尼（Ayatollah Ali Khamenei）舉行長達數日的葬禮。這場預計持續數日的儀式，是在哈米尼於四個月前遭美國與以色列發動空襲身亡後，對該國而言極具意義的時刻。此活動旨在展現儘管戰事持續，伊朗政府依然穩定且受到民眾擁戴。

paragraphs.en: At the Imam Khomeini Grand Mosalla, a large prayer complex in Tehran, thousands of people waited through the night to pay their respects. By early morning, the streets surrounding the mosque were filled with mourners. According to The Guardian, as many as 10,000 people were inside the open-air mosque by 8 a.m. Men and women stood in separate areas, many wearing black to show their sadness. Some mourners beat their chests in a traditional sign of grief, while others chanted slogans against the United States and Israel.
paragraphs.zh: 在德黑蘭的大型祈禱場所「伊瑪目何梅尼大清真寺」（Imam Khomeini Grand Mosalla），數千人徹夜守候以表達哀悼。到了清晨，清真寺周邊街道已擠滿弔唁者。根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，上午八點時，露天清真寺內已聚集多達一萬人。男女分區站立，許多人身穿黑衣以示哀戚。部分弔唁者以傳統方式捶胸表示悲痛，其他人則高喊反對美國與以色列的口號。

paragraphs.en: Khamenei, who was 86 years old, had led Iran for 37 years. The funeral also honors several of his family members who died in the same attack, including his young granddaughter. A spokesperson for the funeral organizers told NPR that the ceremonies were delayed for months because of the difficult conditions of the war. The government hopes the event will send a message of strength to the world, proving that the Islamic revolutionary power that leads the country still has the support of its people.
paragraphs.zh: 享壽八十六歲的哈米尼領導伊朗長達三十七年。葬禮同時也紀念在同一場攻擊中喪生的幾位家族成員，包括他年幼的孫女。葬禮籌備委員會發言人向美國國家公共廣播電台（NPR）表示，由於戰時環境艱困，儀式才延宕數月舉行。政府希望藉此活動向世界傳遞強硬訊息，證明領導該國的伊斯蘭革命勢力仍獲得人民支持。

paragraphs.en: Foreign leaders and religious officials traveled to Tehran to attend the ceremonies. NPR reports that representatives from Russia, Pakistan, Iraq, and Afghanistan were among those present. A delegation from Hamas also attended. Even Saudi Arabia, which has had a difficult relationship with Iran for many years, sent a group to participate. The presence of these international figures highlights the complex political situation in the region.
paragraphs.zh: 各國領袖與宗教官員紛紛前往德黑蘭參加儀式。NPR 報導指出，來自俄羅斯、巴基斯坦、伊拉克與阿富汗的代表皆出席現場。哈瑪斯（Hamas）代表團亦有參加。就連與伊朗關係長期緊張的沙烏地阿拉伯，也派遣代表團參與。這些國際人士的出席，凸顯了該地區複雜的政治局勢。

paragraphs.en: Inside the country, however, opinions remain divided. While many Iranians view the late leader as a spiritual guide, others remember his time in power differently. Khamenei oversaw the Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), which was responsible for the deaths of at least 7,000 people during antigovernment protests that began in late 2025, according to HRANA, an Iranian human rights organization. Some citizens expressed that the funeral brought them little comfort, fearing that the government would continue to use its power against them.
paragraphs.zh: 然而在國內，民意依然分歧。雖然許多伊朗人將這位已故領袖視為精神導師，但其他人對他執政時期的記憶則截然不同。根據伊朗人權組織「伊朗人權活動家通訊社」（HRANA）指出，哈米尼監管的「伊朗革命衛隊」（IRGC）在二零二五年底爆發的反政府抗議中，導致至少七千人死亡。部分民眾表示，這場葬禮帶給他們的慰藉有限，並擔憂政府將持續動用權力對付他們。

paragraphs.en: For those who support the government, the funeral is a way to express their loyalty. The Guardian noted that food stations were set up throughout the city to support the millions of people expected to participate. Some students even offered to take photos of mourners alongside pictures of the new supreme leader, who is the son of the late Ayatollah. Despite the high temperatures, the crowds remained large, with many carrying flags or posters to honor the man who led their country for decades.
paragraphs.zh: 對於政府支持者而言，這場葬禮是表達忠誠的方式。《衛報》提到，市區各處設立了供餐站，以服務預計參與的數百萬民眾。有些學生甚至主動協助弔唁者，讓他們與已故大阿亞圖拉之子、即新任最高領袖的照片合影。儘管氣溫炎熱，人潮依然洶湧，許多人手持旗幟或海報，向這位領導國家數十年的領袖致敬。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the funeral process is expected to continue across several cities. Following the events in Tehran, the body will be moved to other locations to allow more people to pay their respects. At the request of Iraqi officials, the body will also be taken to the cities of Karbala and Najaf in Iraq. This journey is part of a broader effort to emphasize the religious and political importance of the late leader. As the ceremonies continue, the world is watching to see how the Iranian government manages the transition of power and the public's reaction to this long period of mourning.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，葬禮程序預計將在多個城市持續進行。德黑蘭的儀式結束後，遺體將移往其他地點，讓更多民眾能表達敬意。應伊拉克官員要求，遺體也將被送往伊拉克的卡巴拉（Karbala）與納傑夫（Najaf）。這趟行程是強調已故領袖宗教與政治重要性的一環。隨著儀式持續，全球正密切關注伊朗政府如何處理權力過渡，以及大眾對這段漫長哀悼期的反應。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To show honor or affection for someone who has died, often by visiting their grave or attending a funeral.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個固定搭配的慣用語，字面上是「支付尊敬」，實際上指參加喪禮或祭拜。文中：At the Imam Khomeini Grand Mosalla, a large prayer complex in Tehran, thousands of people waited through the night to pay their respects.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To be in charge of a group, organization, or activity and make sure it is done correctly.
keyTerms.noteZh: 容易誤認為是「看過」（oversee 的過去式），這裡指掌管或監管某個組織。文中：Khamenei oversaw the Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), which was responsible for the deaths of at least 7,000 people during antigovernment protests that began in late 2025, according to HRANA, an Iranian human rights organization.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To make one's intentions, feelings, or power clear to others through an action.
keyTerms.noteZh: 並非指真的寄送訊息，而是指透過行動來展現態度或實力。文中：The government hopes the event will send a message of strength to the world, proving that the Islamic revolutionary power that leads the country still has the support of its people.

sources.name: NPR News
sources.title: Dayslong funeral for slain Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei begins in Tehran

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: Crowds gather as six-day funeral for former Iranian supreme leader begins

previousArticles.title: Iran Prepares for Days of Mourning Following Death of Supreme Leader
previousArticles.summaryEn: Iran is preparing for a massive, multi-day funeral ceremony for the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Millions of people are expected to participate in events across the country to honor the leader who died following a recent war. Foreign dignitaries from several nations have arrived in Tehran to pay their respects. The government hopes this show of unity will strengthen its position in ongoing international negotiations. Meanwhile, the country faces a leadership transition as the late leader's son, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, takes over.

previousArticles.title: Iran Prepares for Massive Funeral Following Death of Supreme Leader
previousArticles.summaryEn: Iran is organizing a multi-day funeral ceremony for the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Millions of people are expected to join the events in Tehran and other cities. Foreign leaders from several countries have arrived to pay their respects. The government hopes these events will show strength during a difficult time. The new leader, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, remains in hiding following injuries from a recent attack.

quiz.question: What is the primary reason provided for the delay of the funeral ceremonies?

options.A: The government needed time to arrange the attendance of international leaders.
options.B: The challenging conditions caused by the ongoing war made it difficult to organize earlier.
options.C: The late leader's family requested a period of mourning before the public events.
options.D: The government was waiting for the new supreme leader to officially take office.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，發言人向 NPR 表示，葬禮儀式延遲了數個月是因為戰爭帶來的困難條件（difficult conditions of the war）。選項 A 雖然提到外國領導人出席，但並非延遲的主因；選項 C 與 D 在文中並未提及是導致延遲的原因，屬於無中生有的干擾選項。

quiz.question: Based on the text, what can be inferred about the Iranian government's motivation for the funeral?

options.A: They are primarily concerned with seeking financial aid from the international community.
options.B: They hope to use the event to reconcile with the United States and Israel.
options.C: They aim to project an image of national unity and strength to both domestic and global audiences.
options.D: They are trying to hide the fact that the late leader died in an airstrike.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第一段與第三段均提到，政府舉辦此活動是為了向世界傳達伊朗政權依然穩定且受人民支持的訊息（send a message of strength）。選項 A 文中未提及；選項 B 與文中提到的反美、反以色列情緒矛盾；選項 D 錯誤，因為文章開頭即明確說明死因是空襲，政府並未隱瞞。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the word 'transition' mean in the context of 'the Iranian government manages the transition of power'?

options.A: A physical movement of people from one city to another.
options.B: The process of changing from one leader or system to another.
options.C: A sudden collapse of the existing political structure.
options.D: The act of mourning for a deceased leader.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在此語境下，transition 指的是權力的轉移或交接。文章提到前任領導人過世，其子已成為新的最高領袖，因此這是一個政權更迭的過程。選項 A 是對文中提到的「遺體移動」的誤解；選項 C 與 D 皆不符合「權力」這個搭配詞的語義。

quiz.question: What is the central theme of the article?

options.A: A detailed biography of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's 37-year leadership.
options.B: The logistical difficulties of organizing a funeral during wartime.
options.C: The international diplomatic impact of Saudi Arabia's attendance at the funeral.
options.D: The funeral of the late Iranian leader as a symbolic event amid internal division and regional conflict.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。文章不僅描述了葬禮的舉行，還深入探討了這場活動背後的政治意圖、伊朗國內對於前領導人的分歧評價，以及國際社會的觀望態度，這是全文的核心主旨。選項 A 僅涵蓋部分背景；選項 B 是細節而非主旨；選項 C 僅是文章中的一個小細節，不足以代表全文。
